With the review of the tip formula that we covered in the last session, we have
the benefit, why do it, what is it, how to do it, the result, and the mini dream story.
I’m going to be covering each of these specifically, and giving specific examples,
so there’s no confusion.
Okay, step one is the benefit. This is taken from your USP benefits. We go right
back, again, to the power of your USP. You’ve got 3 benefits there, so you’re
going to take the benefits and your tips are going to all come from there.
Okay, avoid. It’s going to be your second or third benefit, or the problem that
these benefits address.
So, enjoy is going to be your second or third benefit, if you use the second one in
avoid, you’re going to use the third one in benefit. And sometimes, you don’t
even use one of your benefits in the avoid tips section, you just want to use what
they need to avoid. Okay?
And usually, what they need to avoid is the opposite of the benefit that you’re
addressing.
But the key here is that you’ve already got the benefits listed out. You’ve already
got stuff that you know that your prospect wants. These are the top 3 values that
you provide, so why not use them?
And then, the how-to is your primary benefit, the #1 benefit in your USP, the
primary benefit. And, again, this is the most important of the tips.
Examples? Specific examples to follow. We’re just giving you the formula right
now, then we’re going to go into specific examples.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 20 --

2
Step 2: why do it?
What does that mean? You say something to this effect: “This is awesome, if
you really want to blank.” This is awesome, if you really want to throw the most
awesome kids’ party you’ve ever thrown. Or, this is awesome if you want to lose
more weight than you’ve ever lost before.
So, this is very simple. Why do it? Because you’re going to give them the
benefit of doing it.
Let’s use weight loss again, because it’s easier for me. Let’s use something
specific. If it’s something like my tip is you want to eat brown rice, rather than
white rice, this is an awesome thing to do if you really want to control your sugar
metabolized slower than white rice. It can be very specific, like that.
But usually, these tips are very general. It’s awesome, if you want to lose weight.
It’s awesome if you want to increase your business, increase your moneymaking
power online, etc.
This explains why they should listen. Again, that’s very key to this whole thing.
You want to tell someone why they should listen to you. Just to say, “I’ve got a
great tip for you; do this,” and then you go into the tip, you’ve established rapport.
That’s great. But this helps reestablish the rapport by emphasizing what it’s
going to give to them for listening.
Notice how we’re doing this the entire section of every section of your VSL.
We’re doing it over and over again. We’re explaining why you want to do
something. What benefit is it going to give you? This is the secrets behind the
winning sales letter.
The explanation should just be 2 sentences, at the most. It can be only one.
Like, for example, in my example above. “This is awesome, if you really want to
master the dating scene.” That’s all you have to say. This is why you want to do
it.
Okay, examples are going to follow this.
What is it? This is what we call the driving force. This is the reason why it works.
How does this work? In other words, why is this working, to begin with? What is
it and how does it work?
So, you want to focus on making this reason why one short sentence. This is the
ideal place for you to coin a phrase that you can anchor to your product. It’s not
mandatory to coin a phrase; but if you ever wanted to coin a phrase, especially if
your what-is-it is not unique. You can coin a phrase to make it unique.
So, when you see the examples that I’m about to show you, it’s going to
completely click in your head what this means.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 20 --

3
So, don’t worry, we’re just going over the steps right now. But this is the place to
coin a phrase, and it’s the driving force, the reason the tip works to begin with.
Now, step 4: how do you do it?
This is your step-by-step explanation of what’s entailed in the tip. There’s really
nothing more to it than that.
You want to make it 2 to 3 steps, at the most. For example, if I say, “I want you
to avoid white rice.” I’m making this up, of course. “If you want to lose weight,
you want to avoid white rice. You want to substitute it with brown rice. The
reason why you want to do this? This is going to really, really help you lose
weight, because it’s going to control your blood sugar.”
What is the driving force? It controls your blood sugar. That’s why it works.
So, I’ve done this in just 2 sentences. Right? Eat brown rice instead of white
rice. Why do you want to do it? Because it’s going to control your blood sugar.
It’s going to help you lose weight.
So, that’s the driving force. I’ve gone all the way up to step 4 right now, in 2
sentences.
So, I can say, “Here’s exactly what’s entailed in this tip. I can make this very
simple. All you have to do is substitute all your white rice for brown rice. You
just go to the whole grain section of your supermarket and make sure that you
pick whole grain brown rice. And you may want to soak the rice overnight, to
make sure the gluten in the brown rice is gone down to the lowest and that your
digestion improves.”
I’m just making this up, but you get the idea. That’s all you have to do. This is
how you do it. If it’s something that easy, not much more to go into. That’s just a
very valuable tip.
So, in case you have something that is a little more complex, there’s a step
structure that you can take, on these 2 to 3 steps, and it’s simply this: first you
do this, here’s why you do it, and here’s the result.
So, for example, in my silly brown rice analogy, first you do this. What do you
want to do? You want to go to your grocery store and get the brown rice out of
the whole grain canisters. You don’t want to get the packaged stuff. The reason
why you want to do this is because the packaged stuff is instant, and that really
messes up your blood sugar. If you do this right, your blood sugar’s going to be
stabilized. And if you do it wrong, you get the cheap stuff, your blood sugar’s
going to be just as bad as if you eat white rice.
Now, I just rattled that off of the top of my head, simply using the step structure.
So, pretty simple stuff.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 20 --

4
Keep this tip to 2 to 5 sentences, at the most. You can go over, if you absolutely
have to. Like if it’s a really detailed tip, then you may have to go over that. But
there’s no reason to go past 2 to 5 sentences. We’re not talking about a lot of
writing here. We’re talking about giving them value, giving them a little bit vague,
incomplete value, but in some cases specific, like my brown rice example,
something that wouldn’t necessarily prevent someone from buying your product.
But you want to keep it short; 2 to 5 sentences is fine.
Now, I’m going to give you more specific examples here, in just a second.
Step 5 is the result.
Once you do this, the name of the tip, once you substitute brown rice for white
rice, blank will happen.
Now, I kind of covered it in the tip itself. But I’m actually going to expand on it a
little bit here.
If you do it right, more on that in just a moment.
So, here’s what I would do with this tip. Once you substitute bad foods for good
foods – you wouldn’t want to do that… Once you substitute your good foods for
all the bad foods that you’re eating, what’s going to happen is you’re going to
have a ton more energy and you’re going to feel like working out, if you do it
right. If you do it right. There’s more to it. I’m going to cover that in just a
moment.
So, all I’m doing here is opening a little bit of a loop in the conclusion of the tip. If
you do this right, blank will happen. Or, if you do the tip, blank will happen. That
is the result.
I’m going to give you some specific examples here, in a minute.
And then, the mini dream story. This is really the most powerful part. This is a
shortened version of the reader’s dream story, but it’s only one or 2 sentences
long. So, you don’t have to write that whole dream story sequence again. That’s
really good. Right?
So, imagine being able to blank. That’s really all you have to say. Imagine being
able to blank.
Imagine being able to eat the right foods for your body, to control your blood
sugar, so that you lose weight effortlessly. That’s it. That would be plenty. I’m
using one sentence, and I’m just taking one key aspect that the reader can relate
to. That’s the key, one key aspect. So, you don’t ask them to absorb too much.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 20 --

5
Imagine being able to blank, whether that’s walk into a hallway and know that
you can meet any girl that walks down the hall. Imagine being able to throw a
party anytime, and not have to worry about blank, throwing a party, whatever
your service may be. It doesn’t matter, the product or service. You want to walk
them through a little mini dream story.
Now, that’s it. That’s the formula. And it falls within a 3-tip loop.
Remember, we talked about this earlier. This is a breakdown of how you start
your tips and the order that you want to follow.
You’ve got 4 sections here.
Open your how-to loop. Remember, we’re going to start with an open loop. This
is very, very important. Opening this loop – I’m going to give you the exact
structure to do this here, in just a moment – is vital for the entire tip section.
You’re going to open it and you’re going to close it at the end.
Then, you’re going to go into your avoid tip, and right after your avoid tip you’re
going to go right into your enjoy tip. And right after that, you’re going to close this
how-to loop. And that close is going to lead into your pitch or offer, which we’ll
cover in the next session.
But this is incredibly important, to structure your tips in this format. So, open the
how-to loop, go into the avoid tip, go into the enjoy tip, and then close the how-to
loop. That’s the 4 sections.
Now, I’m going to give you very, very specific details here, in the coming slides.
Section One: 	Open Your How-To Loop
What do I mean by that? An open loop is an NLP (Neuro-Linguistic
Programming, if you don’t know that). Basically, you’re covering a subject, and
then you pause to continue it later. But you’re using specific language patterns.
I’ll give you an off-the-cuff example.
We’re going to cover exactly how to do your PowerPoint slides in a way that
people will hypnotically glued to. I’ll cover that in just a moment. But first…
That’s all an open loop is. I just opened a loop. And you’re going, “Oh, I really
want to know that. Oh, he’s going to get to it later.”
The whole “he’s going to get to it later” does what? It forces the reader to keep
watching your video. How important is that? That is so vital to a winning sales
letter, to encourage your viewer to keep watching.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 20 --

6
When you open a loop, if you’re familiar with music at all, it’s kind of like doing a
scale and then ending on the seventh note of the scale. You just want to hit that
last note so badly., because it’s like there’s this open pattern here.
The old Bugs Bunny cartoon, when they used to play the song, he would always
end on the wrong key. You just want to hear this loop closed. Right? Or not
play the next note.
That’s kind of a similar aspect. But the open loop is a very powerful,
psychological way to encourage your reader to keep watching and to give them a
reason to keep watching and to give them a reason to keep watching, and also to
intrigue them. You’re building intrigue throughout your whole presentation.
So, once I tell you the really big thing, and that’s why your how-to loop is, by the
way, the how-to section, the how-to tips is the biggest part of your tips, it’s the
most important, is because this is the one you’re going to open and close later.
When you close it, there’s going to be this sense of relief that’s going to take
them right into your product. So, you are anchoring the concept of relief with
your product. Does that make sense?
So, you’re creating an open loop, which causes tension, then you go into other
tips, then you’re going to remind them of that loop in the how-to section of this
whole process, and then you’re going to close it.
When you close it, it creates just like when you hit that eighth note, that last note,
it closes something. It causes a peace to come over you. And that’s great,
because you’re going to walk right into your product, at that point.
That’s a very powerful psychological state for any potential state for any potential
buyer to be in.
So, here’s a how-to loop.
The most important tip for your reader’s primary goal is the primary benefit.
Okay? Whatever your primary benefit is.
The most important tip for anyone wanting to lose weight effortless, is knowing
how to eat your favorite foods and still drop weight. That would be mine. That’s
my actual literal primary benefit in one of my products, is to be able to eat your
favorite foods and still lose weight.
So, what is my reader’s primary goal? They want to lose weight. What is my
primary benefit? You get to do it by eating your favorite foods.
So now, I’m going to give them a tip on how to do that. But I’m not, really. I’m
going to give it to them later. I’m going to open the loop right now.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

7
So, here’s what we’ve got.
The most important tip for anyone seeking to lose weight and to enjoy the
process – I’ll often include that phrase – is to learn the secret of eating your
favorite foods every single week.
Now, that’s great, right? Everybody’s really intrigued by this.
I’ll go on. I’ll say, “You absolutely have to do this, if you really want to lose weight
and keep it off for the rest of your life, because no one wants to be on a diet all
the time. Right?”
“The reason this works, this is where it gets really interesting, the reason this
works to help you lose weight is because of your,” and this is where the driving
force or coined phrase is going to go.
I’m going to give you my example of a coined phrase, and you’ll just be wanting
me to say, “Oh, keep going. I want to know about that.” And that’s the whole
idea.
“So, the reason this works is because of,” and I’ll tell you, in just a minute, what
my coined phrase is, and then, “I’ll tell you more about that in just a moment.”
Okay, that is opening the loop. You’ve just opened a loop.
So, again, let’s go through these steps.
The most important tip for your reader’s primary goal is your primary benefit. So,
you already know what their primary goal is. That’s up to you, right? The
primary goal of your potential buyer is blank.
And then, you already know what your primary benefit is, because you defined it
in the USP sections, in the USP factory.
So, the most important tip or primary goal is primary benefit.
You want to say something to this in the next sentence: “You absolutely must do
this, if you really want to blank.” And that’s what that primary benefit does.
Whatever your primary benefit does for your reader, if you really want to do that,
you absolutely have to do this tip.
The next sentence is, “The reason this works so well to,” primary goal again, “is
because of” the driving force or the coined phrase.
In other words, why the tip works. Remember the formula? Why does it work?
What’s the driving force behind it? There has to be a reason why this works. If I
tell people, I’m going to make this up, because it’s not true at all, just in case
anyone’s listening that wants to lose weight, the most important tip I could give
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

anyone for wanting to lose weight is to stand on one leg. That’s it. You want to
stand on one leg. You absolutely must do this, if you really, really want to lose
weight and really have fun doing it, and look like a total idiot at the same time.
And we all want to do that, right?
“The reason why this works to help you lose weight and look like an idiot is
because of the one-leg hip-hoppity factor. I’m going to tell you more about the
one-leg hip-hoppity factor in just a moment.”
Now, that is a ridiculous example. I’m going to give you real examples. But
that’s how easy it is once you get this formula down. It’s pretty simple.
So, that is opening the how-to loop, and that is the first part of this section.
There’s 4 parts in this section.
Now, let me give you an example of how-to loop opens that are real. I wanted to
give you some funny ones, first.
This is the real stuff.
“The most important tip that I can give you, if you want to lose weight without
suffering, is to include your favorite foods while you do it. Yes, even pizza, ice
cream and burgers, if you like. You absolutely must do this. If your really want to
lose weight without suffering through deprivation or social isolation. The reason
this works to help you lose weight is based on a very scientific principle we use
exclusively in the Every Other Day Diet, called ‘The Caloric Staggering Principle.’
I’m going to tell you more about that in just a moment. But first…”
And that’s how you lead into the very next section, which is your avoid tips.
That’s all there is to it. I’ve opened a loop, and I’ve done it using every technique
that I’ve just showed you.
So, we’re going to walk over to this page right now, and make sure that you
understand every section that I’ve got highlighted in bold or italics.
“So, the most important tip that I can give you, if you want to lose weight,” that is
my goal. You would substitute your reader’s primary goal there. “Without
suffering,” I included that phrase because it sounded good. My primary benefit is
to include your favorite foods.” And then, I spell out what those favorite foods
are. In other words, I make the sentence just a little juicier. I say, “Pizza, ice
cream, tofu, whatever you like.” I keep it very open-ended.
The next section is I tell them why they must do it. “You absolutely must do this,
if you want to lose weight without suffering through deprivation and social
isolation. Because when people eat rice cakes and whatever, rice cakes and
tofu every single day, to lose weight, or green drinks, do you think that they’re
enjoying social benefits of going out and partying with their friends or having fun

9
at a restaurant? No. they’re stuck at home. Do you think they’re depriving
themselves? Yes. They are.”
So, I’m solving that suffering by giving them my primary benefit.
So, your primary benefit is solving your reader’s suffering. And that’s why they
absolutely must do this tip. “You absolutely must do this, if you really want to”
whatever your benefit is, and why it solves the problem.
Then, I’m going to tell them the reason it works. Again, this is the driving factor
behind why. How on earth can you eat your favorite foods? Well, there’s a
reason why. There’s a reason why this works. It all comes back down to what I
call “The Caloric Staggering Principle,” and it really is my principle in the Every
Other Day Diet.
So, it’s a scientific principle, and it’s completely backed by science, which I go
into within the tip itself. But then, I open the loop. “I’ll tell you more about that
Caloric Staggering Principle and just a moment.
You see how this works?
So, what I want you to be thinking about, as we go through this section, is how
would you put this into your own product? What is your product’s unique benefit,
your primary benefit? Why must someone do this? What’s the reason it works?
And that’s all you have to think about. That’s the 3 steps.
Everyone can use this. “I’ll tell you more about that in just a moment,” or “I’ll tell
you more about the Caloric Staggering Principle in just a moment.” That’s how
you end the loop.
Then we’ll say, “But first,” you want to go into what you want to avoid.
This is section 2, avoiding. And that is the avoid tip structure.
This structure looks like this: the benefit of avoiding blank, whatever it is you’re
going to tell them to avoid, why they want to avoid it, what is it (obviously, you
have to tell them what it is), how to avoid it, the result from avoiding it, and the
mini dream story of their life after avoiding it. In other words, this is the same
formula that we’ve already showed many times throughout this presentation. But
I wanted to make it specific to the avoiding structure.
So, it’s the benefit of avoiding blank. Why avoid it, what is it, how to avoid it, the
result, and the mini dream story.
Let’s give you some specific examples.
I’m going to go back to my weight loss example, just to give you an example.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 20 --

10
If you really want to start dropping weight faster, you absolutely must avoid
eating this specific type of food in the early to late evening.
Notice how I don’t say what food it is, yet? I say, “This specific type of food in the
early to late evening.” So, I don’t tell them what it is. Remember, it’s vague but
incomplete.
I’m actually opening a little miniature loop here, by the way, for those of you who
are into NLP.
And then, the next slide would say, “If you decrease or eliminate this food, this
particular food at night, your insulin levels will come down and you’re going to
store less fat.”
And then, I’ll tell them what the food is. That’s the what is it section. So, here’s
what I’ve done.
I started with what is the benefit. If you really want to start dropping weight
faster, that is the benefit of avoiding this specific type of food. And then, I tell
them why they want to avoid it, why they want to do this tip at all. Because if you
decrease or eliminate this food, your insulin levels are going to go down and
you’re going to store less fat.
Then, I tell them what it is. The food? Carbohydrates, in the form of starches,
rice, breads, potatoes, and so on. That’s what it is.
So, rather than starting off with what it is – in other words, if you really want to
drop weight faster, stop eating carbohydrates at night – I elongate the tip
because this creates more intrigue.
Each time you see a sentence, by the way, think of that as a slide in your video
sales letter. We’ll go into that in the third section of this course.
But this breaks everything down nice and neat. You can see, hopefully, every
single pattern that I’m following. “If you really want to start dropping weight,
that’s the benefit. “If you decrease or eliminate,” that’s the reason why they want
to do it, because they’re going to store less fat. And what is it, the specifics.
“Well, you have to stop eating carbohydrates at night, basically.
So, that’s an avoid tip example.
Now, here’s another one: The best way to avoid the fat gain associated with
even healthy carbohydrates is 3-fold. I said this is another one. This is actually a
continuation of the same one.
The best way to avoid the fat gain associated with even healthy carbohydrates is
3-fold: 1) you want to make sure to eat these foods earlier in the day; 2) you
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

11
want to load up on healthy fats and proteins at night, so you don’t crave
carbohydrates; and 3) you want to substitute these carbs with green vegetables.
Okay, notice that that was the how-to section of this tip. That’s the how-to. In
other words, remember what we were talking about, you want to give 2 or 3 steps
on how to do it.
So, how do you avoid carbs at night? Actually, you could just say, “You just don’t
eat them,” but I went ahead and broke it down, using the formula.
So, how would I avoid eating healthy carbs at night? First, I would eat them
earlier in the day. That’s a good suggestion, by the way.
Second, load up on fat on protein at night, so that I’m less hungry and I don’t
crave the carbs.
And then if I do eat carbs, I’ll eat green vegetables. That’s how you do the tip.
That’s the how-to section, right there, a very simple example.
Now, what happens if they do it? If you avoid starchy carbs at night, except for
certain nights, which I’ll cover in a moment…
Notice that? “Except for certain nights.” So, I open a loop here again. Also,
what am I doing? I’m being vague, like we talked about earlier. I’m not being so
specific.
So, if you avoid starchy carbs at night, that’s a great tip, and then I get vague,
“Except for certain nights, which I’ll cover in a moment, you will not only burn
more fat, but you’ll also decrease your sugar cravings through the day, too. And
daytime sugar cravings is one of the biggest diet killers there is. That’s why you
want to do this.”
Why do they want to do the tip? Because it’s one of the biggest diet killers, is
daytime sugar cravings. And if you do this, you’ll reduce them.
And finally, I end up with the mini dream story.
Just imagine how effortlessly the weight comes off, how much more energy you
have as you beat those sugar cravings for good, just by eating the right foods at
the right time.
That’s just 2 sentences, separated by an ellipsis. That’s it. That’s the mini
dream sequence.
You see how this is working? I tell them what to do, and then I tell them why they
want to do it. And then we come back into this section right here. This is a very,
very important section.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 20 --

12
Okay, we sum it all up. We wrap a bow around it, with a big reason why. Well,
the big reason why is this: this is the reason why I really want you to do it.
So, we’ve given them 2 reasons why. We’ve given them a teaser reason why at
the very start, and we’ve given them the big reason why at the very end.
And then, we go into the mini dream sequence. “Imagine how effortlessly the
weight comes off.”
Notice how I don’t say, “Imagine how effortlessly you’re going to eventually start
losing weight”? No. I’m literally talking to them in the present tense. Dream
stories are best done in the present tense.
So, I say, “Just imagine how effortlessly your weight comes off. I could say
“your” or “the,” how much more energy you have. You see, I’m walking someone
through an imagination state. And that deactivates that decision-making mode
and puts them into desire mode.
So, we do this continuously throughout the video sales letter.
That’s the avoid tip example.
Let’s get on to the enjoy tip. This is section 3, and this is the same exact
structure. I just changed this for your benefits.
So, the benefit of having fun by doing what? Blank. Whatever the tip is. Why
you’ll enjoy it, what it is, how to enjoy it, the result from enjoying it, and then the
mini dream story, your life after you enjoy it. This is the same exact formula. I
just substituted the word “enjoy.”
So, here’s an example.
Now, I want to share another great weight loss tip with you. It’s how you can
actually have fun – yes, have fun – on your journey to a thinner you.
What is that? If you look at this back in the segment here, that’s the benefit of
having fun by blank. In other words, I’m telling them why they’re going to enjoy it.
Notice it says what’s the benefit, and I’m going to tell them why. Okay?
So, let’s look at this again.
The benefit, I want to share a great weight loss tip with you. It’s how you can
have fun on your journey to a thinner you. The benefit is having fun. It’s that
simple.
So, what’s the benefit? It’s having fun to a thinner you. That’s the benefit.

Why do they want to do it? “If you do this, you will not only enjoy the process of
becoming thin that much more, but you’ll also inspire your friends and family to
join in, if they want to. That’s a good reason to do this tip. Right? A reason why
they want to do it.
So, what is it? This is the what-is-it section.
What’s the secret? That’s also a great way to go into this, by the way.
What’s the secret? Refuse to eat anything you don’t absolutely love. Now, that
may sound too obvious, but did you know that 83% of people surveyed said that
in order to lose weight, they believed they had to eat bad-tasting foods. Well,
you don’t; and you should never do it.
That is the how-to. The what-it-is is a little challenging, and the how-to is a little
bit challenging as well. But this was a really ideal place to put in some statistic.
If you have a statistic with your tip, you want to use it. If you have scientific
evidence behind something, you want to use it. If you have peer review
evidence, you want to use it.
This is a great place to look at it.
So, the secret to this is eating foods that you absolutely enjoy all the time, even if
they’re healthy foods.
Now, I’m going to tell them how to do this in the next part of this tip. As you
might imagine, the how-to is coming up.
This is the what it is. What is this thing? What is it? This is it. This is exactly
what I want you to do. I want you to refuse to eat foods that you don’t absolutely
love or enjoy. And 83% of the people surveyed said that in order to lose weight,
they believed they had to eat bad-tasting foods.
Well, that’s a true stat, and that’s what I’m getting them to avoid. So, I’ve already
built in a reason why they should listen to me.
And then, I go into the how-to section.
Here’s how you pull it off. “Tonight, make a list of all of your favorite healthy
foods. Think of it this way: if you had to eat healthy, what would your favorite
food choices be? Lean beef? Chicken breast? Tofu? Salads? Fruits and
vegetables?” Whatever.
And then, I go into the specifics. “Just make a list of 20 of the foods that you
enjoy eating, that are also good for you. Then, refuse to eat anything that is not
on that list. Refuse to eat anything that’s not on the list, with the exception,” and
this is where I open up another loop, “with the exception of your favorite non-
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 20 --

14
healthy foods. Don’t worry, I’m getting to that part. So, keep watching and find
out how you do it.”
That is the how-to section. So, notice how I put in another open loop. You don’t
have to, but it’s a good idea to do it.
So, I’m going back to that how-to tip that we started with. The how-to tip is the
most important, and I’m going to keep reminding them of that. Okay?
Now, I work it right into here. They make a list of 20 foods that they’re going to
enjoy eating, and they’re going to refuse to eat anything that’s not on that list,
even if it’s broccoli. And they know broccoli is good for them and they don’t like
it, don’t eat it.
“With the exception of your favorite non-healthy foods, like pizza, ice cream,
burgers. Don’t worry, I’m going to get to that part in a moment.”
So, what are they going to do? They’re going to keep watching, because they
want to know how they can eat pizza and lose weight.
So, I’m working a how-to and an open loop at the same time.
I’m going to leave these examples up, and we’ll come back to them in just a
moment, so we can get clarity on it.
Here’s how we conclude.
“Let me tell you, once you do this, you will feel more freedom than ever, when it
comes to eating healthy. You feel more in control, and you have a lot more fun
preparing foods you know are really the best for you.”
That’s the reason why they want to do it. This is the reason why, and then I
conclude with the imagine, the dream sequence. “Just imagine never, ever
having to eat foods you dislike, ever.” It’s just one sentence. Just imagine never
having to eat foods you dislike.” That’s also in the present
“So, you never have to eat foods you dislike,” and I tell them exactly what the
benefits are for doing it, the result of doing it. “The result of doing this is that you
will feel more in control and you will have a lot more fun preparing your foods.”
Really, pretty simple. And then, I walk through the dream sequence, and that is
never having to eat foods they dislike.
This structure works for every tip you’ll ever want to come up with.
And we’re going to end up with the how-to tip structure. This is the most
important.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 20 --

15
So, the very first thing we’re going to want to do is close that loop we opened at
the very first. You opened the loop. In my example, I said, “That whole Caloric
Staggering Principle, I’ll tell you about that in just a moment.” Now, we’ve built
up the tension. We’ve told them what to avoid, we’ve told them why, we’ve given
them a list of things to enjoy, we’ve told them why it’s going to make their life
better. And now, we’re going to close this loop by giving them a specific how-to
tip.
We’re going to start with the benefit, just like we did before. Except this time,
we’re going to have the intro to close the loop. Why do you want to do it, what is
it, how to do it, the result from doing it, and the mini dream sequence.
It’s the same thing on every single one of these tips, with the exception of the
intro. That’s it. Very simple structure.
Now, here’s what I mean by closing the loop.
“Here’s the most important weight loss tip of all.” Remember, I said you want to
make this the most important one of all. Right? “If you do not do this, your
weight loss will come to a grinding halt, even if you put all those other tips to work
for you.”
So, I’m really building up a lot of tension here. “What is this most important
weight loss tip? It’s what I mentioned earlier, the Caloric Staggering Principle.”
Okay, now the loop is closed. The moment I say, “It’s what I told you about
earlier, the fill-in-the-blank.” Whatever your driving force is, whatever your coined
phrase is, whatever that is, this is the reason why your primary benefit works.
Mine is the Caloric Staggering Principle. So, we’re going to go back through that.
So, I’m going to intro this by saying, “This is the most important tip of all. If you
do not do this tip, all the other tips are basically useless.” That’s how you want to
phrase this. I just put it into specific words for this specific tip.
“It’s what I told you about earlier. It’s what I mentioned earlier, the blank.”
Once you say the blank, you’ve closed the loop, assuming you go into what the
blank is, and we will.
So, “It’s what I mentioned earlier, the Caloric Staggering Principle, and it’s the
best way to structure your diet so that you actually have to eat your favorite foods
several times a week.”
That’s a pretty good benefit for doing it, right? You actually have to eat pizza.
You actually have to eat your favorite foods? Wow, that is a huge benefit! And
that’s step #1 of this principle. In this case, step 2.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 20 --

16
So, the tip formula begins with benefit. But this formula begins with that intro to
close the loop.
So, I’ve closed the loop with the previous slide and this slide, “It’s what I
mentioned earlier, the Caloric Staggering Principle.”
Now, I’m going to give them the benefit for doing it. “This is the best way to
blank.” In your case, this is obviously going to be different. But in my case, it’s
going to be, “The best way to structure your diet so that you actually have to eat
your favorite foods.”
Now, I’m going to tell; them why they want to do it. “Do this, and you can not
only enjoy your absolute favorite foods, no matter what they are,” notice the
vague sentence there, no matter what they are, just in case someone has the
favorite food of tofu, we don’t know, “but you will actually increase your fat-
burning metabolism. This is the reason why you really want to do this.”
And now, we go into the how-to. “This is how you do it. Here’s how it works.
You want to eat one to 2 days on low-calorie, low-carb meals, then follow that by
one day of higher-calorie, higher-carbohydrate meals. That’s my whole Caloric
Staggering Principle. It’s very simple.”
But, I’m going to tell them the reason it works, and I’m going to use science.
Remember, if you use science and if you can quote science or if you can quote a
study, or if you can quote something empirical, great. Do it.
So, I say, “The reason this works to increase your metabolic rate has to do with a
cool little hormone called T4. T4 decreases after about 72 hours on a low-calorie
starvation diet. And that’s why you should never follow one for more than a few
days. But after one or 2 days of decreased carbohydrates and calories, you can
eat your favorite foods just at the right time, and that is crucial.”
Okay, notice what I’m doing here. I’m telling them, in the how-to section, after
one or 2 days, you decrease your carbohydrates and calories, you can eat your
favorite foods, and then I don’t tell them specifically how to do it.
Remember, vague but useful. It’s very useful that someone learns this right
here, that you can eat one or 2 days on very low calories and one day on a high-
calorie meal. So, if you’re eating higher calories on one day, you can squeeze in
pizza or a burger, or whatever you want. And this is going to work to increase a
hormone called T4, which is going to help increase your metabolism.
Now, that’s all good information, right? But the most vital information is when do
I do this? What do you mean, “At the right time?” That’s the part that I leave out.
That’s the part that I’m going to tell them about in just a moment, when they pick
up my product.
So, this is how you’re guiding someone to the funnel of purchasing your product.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 20 --

17
“So, you can eat your favorite foods just at the right time,” and that is crucial. I’ll
overemphasize that point. “And actually increase your weight loss and your
energy.”
Now, I’ll go into the dream sequence. “Just picture it: planning a fun lunch out
with your friends, knowing that you can enjoy some pizza, a burger, or whatever
you enjoy the most, without feeling the least bit of guilt, because you are actually
helping your body shed those unwanted ugly fat pounds, as you enjoy every little
morsel of food with your friends.”
Now, that’s 2 longer sentences, but that’s all I need. That’s all I need, just a little,
mini dream sequence, a little mini dream story.
Notice how I say, “Picture it, imagine this.” You can say, “Just walk with me for a
minute, and see this. You’re planning a fun lunch out with your friends. You
know that you can enjoy some pizza.” See, I’m talking to them in the present
tense.
This is vital, the whole present tense thing. That is future-pacing, and that is how
you want to do every one of your dream stories.
So, that is the sequence for the tips. And we’re going to go over this in the Q&A,
if we need to. So, no problem if you have questions. Check out the Q&A.
We’re going to get into some advanced tips now, for tips. Now you think, “Oh my
goodness, that was pretty advanced.” Well, these are tip structures that you
want to use. If you get this down and you want to experiment in the future, you
want to consider using these tips for your tips.
First of all, like we talked about, are your tips supported by evidence? If so, you
want to be sure to include that evidence within your tips.
Is this tip relevant to your reader’s greatest desires? Every tip may not be. In
other words, you’ve got to know your reader fairly well. You’ve got to know your
prospect fairly well. Is it relevant to their greatest desire? If it’s not, can you find
one that is?
For now, it’s not super-important that you absolutely get every single one of
them, their greatest desires, but you want to see if it’s relevant. In other words, if
I’m giving someone a tip, “Here’s a great way to burn more calories on a
treadmill,” I can tell you how to do that. But is it going to work within a reader that
is looking for a diet plan? No.
So, you definitely want to make sure that your reader’s greatest desires are being
met, that you’re staying congruent with what you’re offering.
Is your tip unique? This is really the more advanced part of it. If it’s not unique,
you can create a name for it, a catch phrase, like I created Caloric Staggering
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 20 --

18
Principle. I’ve created multiple catch phrases for things that are not unique. In
other words, I didn’t create that principle, that’s just a principle of science, a
principle that goes into thermogenesis and all this kind of scientific stuff.
But the bottom line is up one day, down the next, those kind of things create an
environment for your body to burn more fat. I just gave it a name.
And let me tell you where this comes from.
If you remember the old Budweiser commercials, and if you don’t it’s worth
Googling to look up, they used to say – in fact, they may still say – that their beer
is beechwood aged.
Well, guess what? Every beer on earth is beechwood aged. They just were the
first ones to say it. And because of that, they came off as being unique.
Well, they weren’t unique, they just told people what it is. That’s what they did.
In this case, in my specific example, it’s somewhat unique because most diets
don’t talk about staggering calories. A few do. I was certainly one of the first
people to talk about it – at least that I know of. But nonetheless, I created a
name for it. And you can create a name for whatever your tip is, as well.
Challenge yourself to do that. It’s kind of fun. If you can create and coin a
phrase, even if it’s the same thing everyone else does, if you can coin a phrase,
you’ve got a unique tip.
Now, here’s another advanced tip. You want to think about using provocative
and controversial tips, especially in the void stuff.
In the avoid tips, I love to tell people to avoid stuff that they would think was
going to be healthy for them. So, “These foods that you think are healthy are
really killing you.” That’s pretty controversial.
If I was to say something to the effect of, “You should avoid brown rice, it’s
actually going to make you fat. Yes, I know it’s healthy. Yes, I know it’s good for
you. But you really should avoid it.” That’s provocative.
If you have provocative tips or controversial tips, use them. If your business is
not situated for that, that’s fine. These are advanced tips, again.
So, just something to think about.
Finally, this is one of my favorite, favorite tip-building principles, is to make your
tip as tactile as humanly possible. What I mean by that is you’re talking to people
through a digital medium, a voice in a screen. That’s all you have. You don’t
have touch. You don’t have any of the senses. But I want you to try to think of
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 20 --

19
as many senses as you can. And if you can think of a tip that works in that
sense, use it.
I’ll give you some literal examples. These have all been used in video sales
letters that we’ve created.
“The measure your wrist weight loss tip is one of my favorites. Right now, what I
want everyone to do that’s listening to me, is to take your thumb, take your wrist,
hold your wrist out in front of you, and then wrap your thumb and your middle
finger around your wrist, like you’re creating a bracelet out of your thumb and
middle finger. How far over your thumb goes, it should be over your middle
finger, if possible. If it’s not touching your middle finger, that puts you at a larger
bone size. If it’s overlapping your middle finger, you’re at a smaller bone size. If
it’s barely touching, you’re at a medium bone size.”
So, I can tell people how to adjust their calories based on how far around that
finger goes.
But what have I got people to do? I’ve got people to listen to me and touch
themselves at the same time. It’s the most tactile thing you can imagine,
because you’re literally touching your wrist.
I’ll bet if you’re listening to me right now, you’re touching your wrist. “Oh, that’s
pretty cool. I didn’t know that.”
There’s another way to do this. That’s touch. Let’s use sight.
The cover one eye relationship trick is a great one. It’s very easy to do. Picture,
when you’re talking to someone, when you’re looking at someone that you’re
talking to, if you really want to bond with them deeply, you can picture one of
their eyes is covered and you’re just looking in that other eye.
So, just focus on their left or right eye. It may sound really weird, but try it. Try
looking, right now, at your screen. Try to focus, with both your eyes, at your
screen. If you look at your screen, right now, both your eyes, your screen’s going
to get a little blurry. But if you just concentrate on one word on your screen,
everything comes into focus. That one word comes into hyper-focus.
It’s the same thing with looking in someone’s eye. They can’t tell that you’re
looking in one eye, by the way. It’s a cool little trick to get people to actually get
into deeper rapport with you.
And the other one that I’ve used before is the breathe through one nostril
relaxation technique. So, I will teach people to close their left nostril and breathe
in through their right. And then, they breathe out, they switch nostrils, they close
their left nostril and breathe out through the left. That’s a relaxation/destressing
technique.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 20 --

20
So, I’ll walk someone through a tip like that, and get them to actually go through
the tip with me, and process the tip in a very tactile way. Those are very
powerful ways to get people completely into deeper rapport with you.
Again, if you don’t have a tactile tip in your industry, it’s understandable. If you
do, look for it and try to capitalize on it.
